senses.gloss: to set out on a journey; to travel; to journey; to fare; to venture out or forth

examples.text: ظَعَنْتُ إِلَى دِمْيَاطَ. عَامَ هِياطٍ وَمِيَاطٍ. وَأَنَا يَوْمَئِذٍ مَرْمُوقُ ٱلرَّخَاءِ. مَوْمُوقُ ٱلْإِخَاءِ. أَسْحَبُ مَطَارِفَ ٱلثَّرَاءِ. وَأَجْتَلِي مَعَارِفَ ٱلسَّرَّاءِ.
examples.english: I fared off to Damietta, in a year of hurry-scurry, in arresting luxury, and prized fraternity, pulling along the tails of plenty, relishing visages of felicity.

etymology.text: Cognate to Aramaic טְעַן (ṭəʿan, “to carry”) and Hebrew צָעַן (ṣāʿán, “to wander”).
